plan_summary.primary_goal: Replace the Danish Krone with the Euro within 4–8 years under the Builder's Blueprint, sequencing 24+ months of ERM II convergence before securing the binding referendum and opt-out repeal, all within a DKK 15–25B public-finance envelope.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Tests the DKK 25B budget surplus against bottom-up transition cost; the 8-year timeline margin against projected start-to-Euro-Day duration; the referendum-support margin against the 60% mandate threshold; the deficit-compliance margin against the 3%-of-GDP Maastricht criterion; and the public-awareness margin against the 70% campaign threshold. EU unanimous opt-out repeal, completion of the 24-month ERM II period without exit, and Folketinget passage of enabling treaty legislation are treated as unmodelled existential gates.

key_values.label: Upper bound of the public-finance envelope
key_values.comment: Upper bound of the DKK 15–25B reference range; Issue 2 names inflation erosion over 6–8 years as the dominant real-value risk to this envelope.

key_values.label: Timeline ceiling
key_values.comment: 8-year ceiling from political decision to full euro adoption; slippage beyond 8 years invalidates the Builder's Blueprint and triggers political-fatigue risk per Issue 1.
key_values.source_text: 4 to 8 years from the initial political decision to full euro adoption.

key_values.label: Referendum support threshold
key_values.comment: 60% support is the inferred political floor for a credible Yes mandate; below this the Risk-2 sunk-cost write-off (DKK 500M–1B) materialises.
key_values.source_text: If the referendum support levels drop below 60%, then the vote mandate may be insufficient.

key_values.label: Maastricht deficit criterion
key_values.comment: Deficit must stay below 3% of GDP throughout the ERM II window; Risk 3 names a 6–18 month delay if convergence criteria slip.
key_values.source_text: Failure to meet primary convergence criteria (e.g., deficit < 3% GDP) during ERM II window, delaying ECB approval.

key_values.label: Public-awareness campaign threshold
key_values.comment: 70% public awareness of referendum details is the campaign-pacing trigger; below this the campaign must accelerate to defend the 60% Yes mandate.
key_values.source_text: If public awareness of referendum details reaches less than 70%, then the campaign pacing must be adjusted.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Bottom-up total transition cost in DKK
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Required to test whether the DKK 15–25B envelope absorbs Nationalbank IT integration, public info campaign, cash logistics, EU diplomacy, and the DKK 50M+ ring-fenced contingency over a 6–8 year horizon.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Sum of IT/logistics (Decision 8 buckets) + Shadow Integration Team FTE × 6–8 years + public-info 0.5% × budget + cash-distribution + EU/legal counsel; inflate per Issue 2's real-indexation factor.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved years from political decision to Euro Day
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Drives whether the project clears the 8-year ceiling; Issue 1 names a 12–18 month sequencing delay if EU demands opt-out resolution precede ERM II completion.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Trapezoidal sum of phases: opt-out negotiation + 24+ months ERM II + referendum scheduling + cash logistics; sensitivity to Risk 1 (12–24 month EU delay) and Risk 3 (6–18 month convergence delay).

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved Yes-vote share in the national referendum
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Below 60% the mandate is insufficient (or contested); below 50% the project terminates with DKK 500M–1B sunk-cost write-off.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Pre-referendum polling × turnout × correction for known polling bias on EU questions in Denmark; sensitivity to economic conditions in the 90 days pre-vote.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved peak deficit-to-GDP during ERM II
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Below 3% GDP the deficit criterion holds and ECB convergence approval proceeds; above 3% Risk 3 forces painful last-minute fiscal adjustments and 6–18 month slip.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Project Ministry of Finance baseline fiscal model × business-cycle sensitivity over 24+ months; benchmark against Eurozone accession history for similar small open economies.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved pre-referendum public-awareness share
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Below 70% the Risk 6 'public confusion / inconsistent pricing' failure mode escalates and the 60% Yes mandate becomes vulnerable to last-week swings.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Tracking polls during Public Information campaign + sectoral readiness surveys; sensitivity to campaign spend (0.5% of total budget per Q1 assumption).

recommended_first_calculations.label: Budget surplus against the DKK 25B ceiling
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive surplus preserves the DKK 50M+ contingency and Issue 2's inflation buffer; negative surplus forces austerity that delays Shadow Integration Team funding and cascades into Risk 4 timeline slip.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Timeline margin against the 8-year ceiling
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin keeps the Builder's Blueprint thesis intact; negative margin triggers Issue 1's political-fatigue path and erodes referendum support before the vote can be scheduled.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Referendum support margin against the 60% mandate threshold
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin secures a credible Yes mandate for treaty change; negative margin terminates the project per Risk 2 with DKK 500M–1B sunk-cost write-off.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Maastricht deficit-criterion margin
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin (deficit below 3% GDP) clears the ECB convergence audit; negative margin triggers Risk 3's 6–18 month delay and painful last-minute fiscal adjustments.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Public-awareness campaign margin
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin defends the 60% Yes mandate against late-stage misinformation; negative margin forces the Strategy-11 campaign to accelerate and consumes contingency reserves.

unmodelled_gates.label: EU Council unanimously agrees to amend the TEU protocol lifting the Danish opt-out
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 1 names this as the central treaty-level political dependency; without unanimous EU agreement on the protocol amendment the entire Builder's Blueprint legal sequence stalls regardless of any operational margin the model evaluates.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: 12–24 month delay per Risk 1; political-capital burnout in Copenhagen and Brussels; forced fallback to an uncertain legal pathway with no clear precedent for unwinding a permanent opt-out.

unmodelled_gates.label: Denmark completes the mandatory 24-month ERM II participation without forced exit
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: The minimum 24-month ERM II window is a hard ECB requirement; a speculative-attack-driven exit or DKK realignment during the window resets the convergence clock and adds 24+ months to the timeline regardless of fiscal performance.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Convergence clock restarts; 2+ additional years added to timeline; Risk 8 speculative-pressure scenario materialises with reserve depletion and unfavourable conversion-rate negotiation.

unmodelled_gates.label: Folketinget passes enabling treaty-change legislation by qualified majority
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Danish constitutional practice requires Folketinget approval before the referendum can ratify treaty change; without enabling legislation the referendum cannot be scheduled regardless of EU progress or convergence compliance.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Project termination per Risk 2; DKK 500M–1B sunk costs; return to permanent opt-out and a likely multi-decade political moratorium on euro adoption.
